items.title: Understanding the Clinical Presentation and Urgency of Treatment for HLH
items.content: Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) is a severe hyperinflammatory condition that can lead to multiple organ failure and death if not treated promptly. Both primary (genetic) and secondary (acquired) forms present similarly with sepsis-like symptoms, despite differing underlying causes.
keyPoints: HLH is characterized by massive, uncontrolled inflammation.
Both primary and secondary HLH can lead to multiple organ failure if untreated.
Prompt and appropriate treatment is crucial due to the high risk of mortality.
items.reference: Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis · Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis

items.title: Differentiating Primary from Secondary HLH Based on Demographics
items.content: Primary HLH predominantly affects children, while secondary HLH is more common in adults. This demographic distinction can aid clinicians in considering the likelihood of primary versus secondary forms based on patient age.
keyPoints: Secondary HLH is far more common in adults.
items.reference: Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis · Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis

items.question: Which form of hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis is most common in adults?
options: Both primary and secondary HLH equally
items.explanation: Secondary HLH is far more common in adults.
items.reference: Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis · Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis

items.question: What can trigger secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis?
items.explanation: Secondary HLH is often triggered by infections, malignancies, and autoimmune diseases.
items.reference: Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis · Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis

items.question: What term is used for HLH triggered by autoimmune conditions?
items.explanation: HLH triggered by autoimmune conditions is referred to as macrophage activation syndrome (MAS or MAS-HLH).
items.reference: Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis · Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis

items.statement: The clinical picture of all forms of HLH is quite similar.
items.explanation: All forms of HLH present with a sepsis-like inflammation often leading to multiple organ failure.
items.reference: Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis · Primary and Secondary Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis

items.explanation: Primary HLH mostly affects children, whereas secondary HLH is more common in adults.
